title: Golden Flaky Peruvian Lamb Empanadas
description: Savory lamb empanadas with a splash of brandy and flaky pastry, a halal-friendly showoff Peruvian dish.
instructions: In a skillet over medium heat, sauté onion and garlic until translucent.
Add the ground lamb, breaking it apart, and cook until browned.
Stir in tomato paste, cumin, smoked paprika, and salt; cook for 2 minutes.
Deglaze the pan with brandy, letting the alcohol evaporate for 1–2 minutes.
Pour in lamb broth, bring to a simmer and reduce until the sauce thickens, about 5–7 minutes.
Stir in chopped cilantro, adjust seasoning if needed, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Preheat the oven to 200 °C (400 °F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place a spoonful (about 1–2 Tbsp) of filling onto each empanada sheet, fold into a triangle, and seal edges with a fork.
Brush each empanada with beaten egg.
Bake for 15–18 minutes until golden and flaky, brushing with melted butter halfway through for extra crispness.
